hp.meta.title: Max 60 chars. Front-load primary keyword "resume templates" or "free resume templates". Include brand "Toptal" at end. Add modifier like "ATS-Friendly" or "Free Download" for CTR. Target SERP snippet click-through. Format: [Primary KW] | [Modifier] | [Brand]. Competing with Canva, Resume.io — differentiate with "free DOCX" and "ATS-tested" angles. Secondary KWs to weave in: "cv template", "resume format".
hp.meta.metaDescription: 150-160 chars. Open with action verb + primary keyword "free resume templates". Include secondary KWs: "ATS-friendly", "DOCX download", "Word", "PDF", "Google Docs". Add social proof number ("100+"). End with CTA or unique value prop ("no account required"). Avoid generic phrases — be specific about what user gets. Target KWs: "resume templates free download", "cv template free", "free resume templates". Differentiate from Canva (no login) and Resume.io (instant download).
hp.meta.ogTitle: Social sharing title. Can differ from title tag — optimize for social CTR, not SERP. Use numbers ("100+") and urgency. Include "free" and "resume templates". Keep under 60 chars. Consider emoji or special chars for social feeds. Target: high share/click ratio on LinkedIn, Twitter, Facebook.
hp.meta.ogDescription: Social sharing description. 1-2 sentences max. Lead with strongest value prop (free, no account, ATS-tested). Mention specific ATS systems (Workday, Greenhouse, iCIMS) for credibility on LinkedIn. Include "DOCX download" as differentiator. Target professional audience on LinkedIn primarily.
hp.hero.h1: Front-load primary keyword "free resume templates". This is the single most important on-page SEO element. Keep under 70 chars. Include "ATS-friendly" (48K monthly searches for "ats friendly resume template"). Add "DOCX download" or "instant download" as differentiator. Format as listicle signal: start with number ("100+"). Must match search intent: user wants to browse/download templates, not read about resumes. Secondary KWs to include if possible: "cv template", "resume format". Avoid: generic "professional resume" without action signal.
hp.hero.subtitle: 20-50 words. Expand on H1 with secondary keywords NOT already in H1. Must include: "resume format" (645K vol), "cv template" (535K vol), "Word" and "Google Docs" (format keywords). Mention ATS systems by name (Workday, Greenhouse, iCIMS) for E-E-A-T. Include "recruiter-approved" for trust. Add "no account required" as differentiator vs Canva/Resume.io. This is a listicle page — subtitle should set expectation that user will browse a curated list of templates. Natural reading flow, not keyword stuffed.
hp.hero.trustLine: Under 20 words. E-E-A-T authority signal. Include specific number ("100,000+") for credibility. Mention "Toptal's hiring network" for brand authority. Can include "CV template" as secondary keyword if natural. This line appears prominently — it's the trust anchor. Differentiate from competitors: Canva has no hiring expertise, Resume.io has no recruiter network. Use data-backed language, not vague claims.
hp.galleryHeader.eyebrow: Short section label (2-4 words). Should signal browsing/exploration intent — e.g. "Browse by Category", "Explore Templates". Keep concise — this is a UI eyebrow tag above the H2. Include topical relevance for resume templates browsing.
hp.galleryHeader.title: H2 heading for the template categories grid. Include primary keyword "resume templates". This heading introduces the browsable category grid. Keep under 60 chars. Should convey variety and completeness of the template collection.
hp.galleryHeader.subtitle: 1-2 sentences describing the categories section. Mention number of categories, template variety, and what users can find. Include keywords: "resume templates", "guides", "downloads". Keep under 30 words.
hp.freeTemplatesHeader.eyebrow: Short section label (2-3 words). Should signal free/downloadable nature — e.g. "Free Downloads", "Free Templates", "Complete Guide". Keep concise — this is a UI eyebrow tag above the H2.
hp.freeTemplatesHeader.title: H2 heading for the free templates download section. Front-load "free resume templates" keyword. This heading captures high-intent free download traffic. Keep under 60 chars. Must differentiate from the main gallery section.
hp.freeTemplatesHeader.subtitle: 1-2 sentences describing the free download section. Mention file formats (DOCX, Word, Google Docs). Include keywords: "free resume templates", "download". Mention the builder for upsell. Keep under 30 words.
hp.guide.eyebrow: Short section label (2-3 words). Should signal authority and completeness — e.g. "Complete Guide", "Expert Guide", "Full Guide". Include "guide" for topical relevance. This eyebrow text appears above the H2 and sets the tone for the entire guide section. Keep concise — it's a UI label, not a headline.
hp.guide.title: H2 heading for the guide section. Front-load primary keyword "resume templates". Include secondary KWs: "CV formats", "resume". Target PAA: "What is the best resume template?" This heading wraps the entire 10-chapter guide — it should be comprehensive and authoritative. Include "complete guide" framing for long-form content signal. Keep under 80 chars. Must differentiate from H1 (which is listicle-focused) — this is informational/guide-focused.
hp.guide.subtitle: 1-2 sentences summarizing the guide scope. Include secondary keywords: "resume template", "CV", "ATS", "formatting". Set expectation for comprehensive coverage. Mention outcomes: "passes ATS systems", "lands interviews". Keep under 30 words. This subtitle appears directly under the guide H2 and should encourage users to read the full guide rather than skip to templates.
hp.guide.0.heading: H2 targeting "resume templates vs cv templates" — People Also Ask query. Include both "resume template" and "cv template" keywords. This chapter captures the CV/resume disambiguation intent (cv template = 535K, cv format = 222K). Frame as comparison for international job seekers. Target featured snippet with clear comparison structure. Keep heading under 60 chars.
hp.guide.0.body: 300-500 words. Body type: paragraph + table. Primary KWs: "resume template" (823K), "cv template" (535K), "cv format" (222K). Structure: Open with 2-3 paragraphs explaining the difference between CV and resume for different markets (US vs UK/EU/Asia). Include a comparison table (resume vs CV: length, format, use case, region). Weave in LSI keywords: "curriculum vitae", "job application", "cover letter". Internal link to CV templates chapter. E-E-A-T: cite specific hiring market conventions by region. Target PAA featured snippet with the table.
hp.guide.1.heading: H2 targeting "best resume format" (645K for "resume format"). Include "resume format" as primary keyword. Frame as a "how to choose" guide — matches informational intent. Secondary KWs: "chronological", "functional", "combination" resume formats. Target PAA: "What is the best format for a resume?" Featured snippet opportunity with table or list format.
hp.guide.1.body: 400-600 words. Body type: paragraph + bullet list + table. Primary KW: "resume format" (645K). Cover 3 main formats: chronological (most common), functional (career changers), combination (experienced). Include comparison table: format type, best for, structure, ATS compatibility. LSI keywords: "resume layout", "resume structure", "work experience", "skills-based resume". Bullet list of when to use each format. Include decision flowchart logic in text. Internal link to ATS chapter (ch 6). E-E-A-T: reference recruiter preferences and hiring data.
hp.guide.2.heading: H2 targeting "free resume templates" (133K) + "resume templates free download" (167K). Must include "free", "resume templates", and download format keywords (PDF, Word, Google Docs). This is the core listicle chapter — where users actually browse templates. High commercial intent. Secondary KWs: "cv template free" (59K), "free cv template" (47K).
hp.guide.2.body: 500-800 words. Body type: paragraph + bullet list. This is the CORE commercial chapter — highest conversion intent. Primary KWs: "free resume templates" (133K), "resume templates free download" (167K), "cv template free" (59K). Structure: Brief intro (2-3 sentences), then present template categories with bullet descriptions. Each template mention should include: name/style, best use case, download formats available. Emphasize: free, no account, instant download, ATS-tested. LSI: "professional resume template", "modern resume", "simple resume template". CTA language throughout. This chapter should feel like browsing a curated collection.
hp.guide.3.heading: H2 targeting "resume templates for freshers" and "student resume template". Include "freshers" (high-volume in India/international markets) and "free download". Secondary KWs: "entry-level resume", "no experience resume". This chapter captures the student/recent grad segment. Target PAA: "How to make a resume with no experience?"
hp.guide.3.body: 300-500 words. Body type: paragraph + bullet list + table. Primary KWs: "resume templates for freshers", "student resume template", "entry-level resume". Address the "no experience" anxiety head-on. Structure: Intro acknowledging the challenge, bullet list of what to include instead of work experience (projects, coursework, internships, volunteer work, skills). Table comparing fresher resume sections vs experienced resume sections. LSI: "first job resume", "recent graduate", "college resume", "internship resume". E-E-A-T: mention hiring data on what recruiters look for in entry-level candidates. Internal link to format chapter (ch 1).
hp.guide.4.heading: H2 targeting "cv templates" (42K) and "professional cv templates". Include "CV template" as primary keyword for international audience. Secondary KWs: "cv examples" (65K), "cv sample" (34K), "curriculum vitae" (126K). Frame for UK/EU/international job seekers. Differentiate from resume-focused chapters.
hp.guide.4.body: 300-500 words. Body type: paragraph + bullet list. Primary KWs: "cv template" (42K), "cv examples" (65K), "curriculum vitae" (126K). Write for international audience (UK, EU, Asia, Middle East). Explain CV-specific requirements: length (2+ pages OK), sections unique to CVs (publications, research, references). Bullet list of CV template styles available. LSI: "academic cv", "europass cv", "cv format", "professional cv". Differentiate from resume chapters — this is specifically for markets that use CVs. E-E-A-T: reference country-specific hiring norms. Internal link to ch 0 (CV vs resume comparison).
hp.guide.5.heading: H2 targeting "how to make a resume" — high informational intent query. Include "resume for a job" for long-tail targeting. Step-by-step format targets featured snippet (ordered list). Secondary KWs: "resume example" (45K), "sample resume" (34K). This is the most informational chapter — should link to templates as the solution.
hp.guide.5.body: 500-700 words. Body type: ordered list + callout. Primary KW: "how to make a resume". MUST use numbered steps format — this targets Google's featured snippet for how-to queries. Steps: 1) Choose format, 2) Add contact info, 3) Write summary/objective, 4) List experience, 5) Add skills, 6) Education, 7) Customize for job. Each step: 2-3 sentences. LSI: "resume writing", "resume tips", "job application", "resume example". Include callout box with pro tip from recruiter perspective. End with CTA to download a template to get started. Internal links to format chapter (ch 1) and ATS chapter (ch 6). E-E-A-T: step-by-step authority.
hp.guide.6.heading: H2 targeting "ATS-friendly resume" (78K) and "ats friendly resume template" (48K). Front-load "ATS-friendly" or "ATS" in heading. This is a key differentiator chapter — most competitors don't explain ATS compatibility in depth. Include "resume format" as secondary KW. Target PAA: "How do I make my resume ATS-friendly?" Featured snippet opportunity.
hp.guide.6.body: 400-600 words. Body type: paragraph + bullet list + table. Primary KWs: "ATS-friendly resume" (78K), "ats friendly resume template" (48K). This is Toptal's KEY differentiator chapter. Structure: Explain what ATS is (1-2 sentences), why it matters (70%+ resumes rejected by ATS), then actionable tips as bullet list. Include table of ATS systems (Workday, Greenhouse, iCIMS, Lever) with compatibility notes. LSI: "applicant tracking system", "resume parsing", "resume keywords", "resume scanner". E-E-A-T: mention Toptal's actual ATS testing process. Address common ATS myths. Internal link to format chapter (ch 1) and design chapter (ch 7). This chapter builds trust for conversion.
hp.guide.7.heading: H2 targeting "resume design" and "resume layout" keywords. Include "formatting" for "resume format" (645K) long-tail capture. Secondary KWs: "professional resume" (38K), "resume template" design aspects. This chapter supports the visual/design intent — users who want their resume to look good. Best practices format for featured snippet.
hp.guide.7.body: 300-500 words. Body type: paragraph + bullet list + callout. Primary KWs: "resume design", "resume layout", "resume formatting". Cover visual best practices: margins, fonts, section spacing, color usage, white space. Bullet list of do's and don'ts. Include callout with the #1 design rule (readability > creativity). LSI: "professional resume", "modern resume design", "resume font", "resume margins". Balance aesthetics with ATS compatibility — mention that overly designed resumes fail ATS. Internal link to ATS chapter (ch 6). E-E-A-T: reference design principles used by Toptal's template designers.
hp.guide.8.heading: H2 targeting "resume builder" (319K) — capture users searching for builders and redirect to templates. Frame as comparison: builder vs. template download. Include "resume builder" as primary KW. Secondary: "canva resume" (282K), "canva resume template" (35K). This chapter intercepts builder-intent traffic and converts to template downloads.
hp.guide.8.body: 400-600 words. Body type: table + paragraph. Primary KWs: "resume builder" (319K), "canva resume" (282K). This is a COMPETITIVE chapter — intercepts builder-intent traffic. Structure: Comparison table (builder vs template download) covering: cost, customization, ATS compatibility, time required, output format. Paragraphs explaining pros/cons of each approach. Position templates as superior: more control, better ATS results, truly free, no account lock-in. LSI: "online resume maker", "resume creator", "canva resume template", "resume generator". Don't name competitors directly — compare category ("online builders" vs "downloadable templates"). E-E-A-T: why hiring experts prefer editable templates.
hp.guide.9.heading: H2 targeting download format queries: "resume templates free download" (167K), "cv template word" (67K). Include PDF, Word, and "editable templates" in heading. Target users who have chosen a template and need format guidance. Secondary KWs: "resume template free" (51K), "DOCX". This is a bottom-funnel chapter — users ready to download.
hp.guide.9.body: 300-500 words. Body type: table + paragraph. Primary KWs: "resume templates free download" (167K), "cv template word" (67K). This is BOTTOM-FUNNEL — users ready to download. Structure: Format comparison table (DOCX vs PDF vs Google Docs) covering: editability, ATS compatibility, sharing, universal access. Brief paragraph for each format explaining when to use it. LSI: "resume template Word", "resume PDF", "Google Docs resume template", "editable resume". Emphasize DOCX as the most versatile format. Include download instructions or CTA for each format. E-E-A-T: explain why recruiters prefer certain formats. Internal link to free templates chapter (ch 2).
hp.faqs.0.question: Target PAA: "How do I customize a resume template?" — exact match or close variant. High informational intent. This FAQ should capture the "how to edit/use a template" search cluster. Answer should start with direct 1-sentence response (featured snippet format), then expand with steps. Include "resume template" keyword naturally.
hp.faqs.0.answer: Featured snippet format: Start with direct answer in first 40-50 words. Include "resume template" keyword in first sentence. Mention specific tools (Word, Google Docs) for how-to context. Add Toptal-specific differentiator (e.g., DOCX format works everywhere). Keep total answer 80-120 words. End with implicit CTA pointing to template download. Include "ATS-friendly" if natural.
hp.faqs.1.question: Target PAA: "Are resume templates ATS-friendly?" or "Are free resume templates ATS compatible?" Include "ATS-friendly" and "resume templates" keywords. This FAQ captures the trust/verification intent — users worried their template won't pass ATS. High conversion relevance.
hp.faqs.1.answer: Featured snippet: Start with confident "Yes" + direct explanation in first 40 words. Name specific ATS systems (Workday, Greenhouse, iCIMS) for E-E-A-T credibility. Mention testing methodology — how templates were validated. Include "ats friendly resume template" keyword. Differentiate from competitors who claim ATS-friendly without proof. Keep 80-120 words.
hp.faqs.2.question: Target PAA: "What format should I download my resume in?" or "What file formats for resume templates?" Include "file formats" and "download" keywords. Captures the format-decision intent. Answer should cover DOCX, PDF, Google Docs.
hp.faqs.2.answer: Featured snippet: Lead with the 3 formats available (DOCX, PDF, Google Docs) in first sentence. Explain when to use each format (DOCX for editing, PDF for submission, Google Docs for collaboration). Include "resume templates free download" keyword. Mention "no account required" differentiator. Keep 80-120 words. Include "Word" and "cv template" if natural.
hp.faqs.3.question: Target PAA: "Are these resume templates free?" or "Can I download resume templates for free?" Include "free" and "resume templates" keywords. Captures the cost/trust verification intent — users skeptical about "free" claims. Critical for conversion.
hp.faqs.3.answer: Featured snippet: Start with clear "Yes, 100% free" in first sentence. Emphasize: no account required, no hidden fees, no watermarks. Include "free resume templates" (133K) and "resume templates free download" (167K) keywords. Differentiate from Canva (requires account) and Resume.io (freemium). Keep 60-100 words. End with reassurance.
hp.faqs.4.question: Target PAA: "How do I choose the right resume template?" or "What template should I use for my industry?" Include "choose" and "resume template" keywords. Captures the decision-paralysis intent — users overwhelmed by options on a listicle page.
hp.faqs.4.answer: Featured snippet: Start with simple decision framework in first 40 words (e.g., "Match template style to your industry"). Mention template categories: ATS-friendly for corporate, creative for design roles, simple for entry-level. Include "professional resume" (38K) and "resume format" keywords. Reference the template list on this page. Keep 80-120 words.
hp.faqs.5.question: Target branded query: "What makes Toptal templates different?" or "Why use Toptal resume templates?" Include "Toptal" brand keyword. Captures comparison/evaluation intent. This FAQ is for E-E-A-T and brand differentiation.
hp.faqs.5.answer: E-E-A-T authority answer. Lead with Toptal's hiring network credibility (100,000+ resumes reviewed). Differentiate: real recruiter feedback vs auto-generated templates, ATS-tested across actual systems, free DOCX with no account vs competitors. Include "resume templates" keyword. Mention specific competitor weaknesses without naming them. Keep 80-120 words. Build trust for conversion.
hp.expertise.heading: Include "Toptal" brand keyword for branded search capture. E-E-A-T authority signal — this heading introduces the trust section. Include "hiring expertise" or "hiring network" for topical relevance. Keep under 60 chars. Should convey: why Toptal is uniquely qualified to provide resume templates. Differentiate from generic template sites. Secondary KW: "professional resume" if natural.
hp.cta.heading: Conversion-focused heading. Include primary keyword "resume template" for SEO. Use action verb ("Find", "Download", "Get"). Add urgency or specificity ("Today", "Perfect", "Your"). Keep under 50 chars. This is the last thing users see before converting — must be compelling. Match the listicle page intent: user has browsed templates, now needs to act.
